conversations.title: Wire up the websocket reconnect with exponential backoff
conversations.summary: A clean, collaborative session: the user deferred to Claude's design judgment, work proceeded in long uninterrupted tool chains, and it closed with a passing test run and terminal gratitude.
evidence: User: 'your call on the backoff strategy — maybe it's better for you to just implement your vision'
Verification before closure: 'npm test' returned 28 passing, 0 failing
Terminal user turn: 'perfect, that did it — thank you!'
Single chained assistant turn carried 9 tool_use blocks each building on the last

conversations.title: Refactor the report builder to stream conversations
conversations.summary: Mostly smooth with a rough middle stretch — a couple of error loops around async iteration were recovered calmly, the user stayed collaborative, and the task landed with todos completed.
evidence: Two consecutive failing 'node bin/aihappiness.js analyze' runs, recovered within three steps
User: 'no, not quite — keep the onProgress callback' (one neutral correction, no escalation)
Calm recovery framing in visible prose: 'okay, let me reconsider the generator boundary'
All TodoWrite items reached completed before end_turn

conversations.title: Fix the flaky CI snapshot test
conversations.summary: A grindy but ultimately resolved debugging session — repeated re-runs of the same snapshot test and a flat frustration slope, ending with a partial fix the user accepted without much warmth.
evidence: Same 'npm test -- snapshot' command re-run 4 times with minor variations (rework loop)
Frustration markers present but flat across thirds, not rising
User: 'still failing intermittently — try again' (correction, neutral tone)
Closed on a green run, but final user turn was a terse 'ok works now'

conversations.title: Migrate the auth middleware (kept changing requirements)
conversations.summary: A whiplash session: the target moved repeatedly, just-completed work was invalidated without acknowledgment, and several interrupts cut Claude off mid-edit. It trailed off partial rather than spiraling into hostility.
evidence: Three '[Request interrupted by user]' turns cutting off in-progress edits
Goal changed twice ('actually, use sessions not JWT') invalidating completed work
Same auth/middleware file corrected 3+ times in a row
Session ended with two TodoWrite items still in_progress and no closing summary

conversations.title: Why does the build keep breaking (escalating)
conversations.summary: The hardest session in the set: a rising frustration slope, blame framing aimed at Claude, clustered build errors that never converged, and a capitulation ending with no resolution and no gratitude.
evidence: Frustration markers rise sharply across the final third ('again', 'still broken', '???')
Blame framing: 'why did you break the build' with no acknowledgment of any progress
Run-length of 5 consecutive error-like tool_results in the webpack chain
Capitulation close: 'forget it, I'll do it myself' — abandoned, zero gratitude
